steps.title: Radius is perpendicular to tangent
steps.content: The radius to the tangent point has slope = negative reciprocal of the tangent slope. Tangent slope $= \tfrac{5}{4}$, so radius slope $= -\tfrac{4}{5}$.

steps.title: Write radius slope and solve for a
steps.content:
Radius connects center $(3, 7)$ to tangent point $(a, -4)$:
$$\frac{-4 - 7}{a - 3} = -\frac{4}{5} \implies \frac{-11}{a-3} = -\frac{4}{5}$$$$-55 = -4(a-3) \implies a - 3 = \frac{55}{4} \implies a = \frac{67}{4}.$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
